title: Supersymmetric Approach to the Analysis of Random Band Matrices
speaker: Mariya Shcherbina
abstract: We discuss an application of the SUSY approach to the analysis of spectral characteristics of hermitian and non hermitian random band matrices. In 1D case the obtained integral representations for correlation functions of characteristic polynomials allow to analyze the mechanism of phase transition between “localized” and “delocalized” spectral behavior.

proof_stages.label: Random band matrix model and scaling
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines one- and higher-dimensional Gaussian random band matrices, their bandwidth-dependent variance profile, the Hermitian/non-Hermitian variants, and the special inverse-Laplacian covariance convenient for the later supersymmetric reduction.

proof_stages.label: Spectral observables and transition targets
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates global laws, local correlation functions, GUE/Ginibre and Poisson statistics, localization of eigenvectors, and the expected Anderson-transition thresholds for random band matrices, placing the announced results among previous partial results.

proof_stages.label: Determinantal observables suited to supersymmetry
proof_stages.summary: Ordinary spectral correlation functions are replaced by ratios of characteristic determinants, with Hermitization in the non-Hermitian case, and by characteristic-polynomial correlations that are accessible to supersymmetric integration.

proof_stages.label: Grassmann calculus and determinant identities
proof_stages.summary: Grassmann variables and Berezin integration are introduced so that Gaussian integrals in anticommuting and commuting variables represent determinants and inverse determinants, hence determinant ratios.

proof_stages.label: Gaussian averaging to a statistical-mechanics partition function
proof_stages.summary: Because the matrix entries enter linearly in the supersymmetric exponent, Gaussian averaging converts the determinant-ratio observables into finite-dimensional integrals that, for the chosen covariance, have the form of a nearest-neighbor statistical-mechanics partition function.

proof_stages.label: One-dimensional transfer-operator and resolvent reduction
proof_stages.summary: In one dimension the partition function is written as a power of a generally non-self-adjoint transfer operator, then analyzed via resolvent contours, equivalence reductions, changes to eigenvalue/unitary variables, and representation-theoretic spectral information for a difference operator on the unitary group.

proof_stages.label: Spectral-gap comparison and crossover conclusion
proof_stages.summary: Comparing the transfer-operator spectral gap of order W^{-2} with the local perturbation of order N^{-1} yields the Poisson, GUE/Ginibre, and intermediate regimes, leading to the stated Hermitian crossover results and the announced first non-Hermitian result.
